message: Node started: Implement a local area adjustment routine for a set of non-overlapping rectangles. Input: A set of initial rectangles R = {[x1i, x2i, y1i, y2i]} and a set of target areas A = {ai}. Logic: 1. For each rectangle i, calculate current area Si. 2. Define a target expansion/contraction factor ki = sqrt(ai / Si). 3. Attempt to expand each rectangle i by scaling its dimensions relative to its center (cxi, cyi) by factor ki. 4. If an expansion causes an overlap with a neighbor j, clip the boundary of i to the boundary of j (using the axis-aligned constraint). 5. Use a greedy iterative approach: process rectangles in order of |ai - Si| (descending) and adjust boundaries until no further expansion is possible without overlap. Output: A list of adjusted rectangles R' = {(x1'i, x2'i, y1'i, y2'i)} that are non-overlapping and maximize sum -|S'i - ai|.
